translation: When the sun is folded up,
And when the stars lose their lustre,
And when the mountains are moved,
And when the ten-months pregnant camels are abandoned,
And when the wild beasts are gathered,
And when the seas overflow,
And when the souls are rejoined (with their bodies),
And when the girl-child buried alive shall be asked,
For what sin was she killed?
And when the scrolls [of human deeds] are laid open,
And when the sky is stripped off,
And the Hell stoked,
And the Paradise brought near,
Every person comes to know what he has brought along.
notes: It will be a different world altogether in which we would be brought to life again after our death in this world. With our present knowledge, we are not able to understand fully that other world although we are given its portents in the foregoing Verses of this Chapter revealed during the initial [Makkan] period of Muhammad’s prophethood.

translation: No! [The misgivings of the people that do not believe in these revelations are not correct, and] I swear by the stars
That retreat and hide ,
notes: The allusion appears to be to the stars in the sky that gradually become dim to the human eyes as dawn breaks, and totally disappear at daybreak.

translation: And by the night as it moves,
And the morning as it breathes,
This is indeed the word of a noble [Angel] Messenger.

arabic: ذِي قُوَّةٍ عِندَ ذِي ٱلۡعَرۡشِ مَكِينٖ
transliteration: Thee quwwatin AAinda thee alAAarshi makeenin
translation: A mighty One with the Lord of the Throne;
One to be obeyed besides being trustworthy.
And your companion [Prophet Muhammad] is not mad.
And he did certainly see the Angel Messenger on the clear horizon.
And he [Prophet] does not grudge (conveying what has been revealed to him) of the Unseen.
And it is not a word of accursed Satan.
Where then are you going?
This is nothing but Good Counsel for the worlds .
notes: Refer study note 5 on Verse 1:2 (PART I of this Tafsir).

translation: For any of you who wishes to follow the Straight Way;

arabic: وَمَا تَشَآءُونَ إِلَّآ أَن يَشَآءَ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّ ٱلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
transliteration: Wama tashaoona illa an yashaa Allahu rabbu alAAalameena
translation: And you wish not unless Allah, the Lord of the Universe , so wishes!
notes: Refer study note 5 on Verse 1:2 (PART I of this Tafsir).
